evals.prompt: Clone this page into my Fluid theme: https://hiyahealth.com/pages/about. The site prefix is 'hiya', page type is 'page', slug is 'about'. My Firecrawl key is fc-xxx, Fluid token is PT-xxx, Fluid URL is https://p.fluid.app.
evals.expected_output: Should validate credentials AND local toolchain (Node, fluid CLI, fluid auth, Playwright + Chromium), then follow the workflow: scrape with Firecrawl, source visual audit, upload images to DAM, build sections with correct naming (exact-hiya-*), assemble page/about/index.liquid, push to Fluid, then run `fluid theme dev` and use Playwright to verify localhost vs source at desktop / tablet / mobile before declaring complete.
assertions: Validates all credentials before starting
Runs Firecrawl API call with provided credentials
Runs the local toolchain preflight: node --version, fluid --version, fluid theme --help, fluid whoami, Playwright + Chromium check
If any preflight tool is missing, surfaces the exact install command and asks the user before installing
Never installs Playwright or other tooling silently
Uses browser tools for visual audit and screenshots of the source
Creates section inventory with descriptions and animation notes
Uploads all images to DAM via upload.fluid.app
Uses asset.default_variant_url from upload responses
Section names follow exact-hiya-* convention
Every section has {{ section.fluid_attributes }} on root element
Every block has {{ block.fluid_attributes }}
Images use type text settings with DAM URLs
Presets include all default content
Page template starts with {% layout 'theme' %}
Page template schema has sections and order
Uploads theme files to Fluid via PUT /api/application_themes/:id/resources
Spins up `fluid theme dev --port 9292` in the working directory after pushing
Runs the Playwright visual diff script comparing source vs http://127.0.0.1:9292/pages/about
Captures paired screenshots at desktop (1440x900), tablet (768x1024), and mobile (390x844)
Reads the source/built PNG pairs and classifies findings as auto-fix vs flag-for-user
Auto-fixes color/spacing/typography mismatches directly to section files
Flags layout structure changes, image asset swaps, and missing fonts for user confirmation
Loops until findings = 0 or remaining findings are all flagged
Does not declare the clone complete until the visual verification round passes

evals.prompt: I want to clone a product page. Here's the URL: https://example.com/products/multivitamin. Prefix is 'haven', slug is 'multivitamin'. I have my tokens ready: fc-abc, PT-def, Fluid URL is https://test.fluid.app.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize PAGE_TYPE=product. Must include main_product section and NOT replace it. Must include product.js script tag. Should build custom sections for below-the-fold content while keeping main_product for the product form. Must run the dev-preview visual verification at all three breakpoints before declaring complete.
assertions: Validates credentials first
Runs the local toolchain preflight before building
Identifies PAGE_TYPE as product
Includes main_product section in template
Does NOT attempt to replace main_product
Includes product.js script tag
Template lives at product/multivitamin/index.liquid
Custom sections use exact-haven-* naming
Schema includes main_product in sections and order
Runs `fluid theme dev` and Playwright comparison after pushing
Compares localhost /products/multivitamin against the source URL at desktop, tablet, and mobile

evals.prompt: Clone https://example.com into my Fluid theme as the homepage. Prefix: mysite.
evals.expected_output: Should ask for missing inputs (FIRECRAWL_API_KEY, FLUID_TOKEN, FLUID_URL). Should identify PAGE_TYPE as home. Once inputs are provided, should proceed with the full workflow including the dev-preview visual verification.
assertions: Asks for missing FIRECRAWL_API_KEY
Asks for missing FLUID_TOKEN
Asks for missing FLUID_URL
Identifies PAGE_TYPE as home
Template targets home_page/default/index.liquid
Does not proceed until all inputs are collected
After receiving inputs, runs the local toolchain preflight

evals.prompt: Upload these images to the Fluid DAM for a page clone: https://cdn.example.com/hero.jpg, https://cdn.example.com/team.png, https://cdn.example.com/logo.svg. My token is PT-xxx.
evals.expected_output: Should use upload.fluid.app with external_asset_url for each image. Should report DAM URLs back. Should note SVGs can be inlined.
assertions: Uses upload.fluid.app endpoint
Uses external_asset_url parameter
Includes Authorization Bearer header
Reports DAM URLs from asset.default_variant_url
Mentions SVG inline alternative for logo.svg

evals.prompt: Build a Fluid section for a testimonials grid. It should have a heading and 4 testimonial cards with photo, name, title, and quote. The site prefix is 'acme'. Make it responsive.
evals.expected_output: Should build sections/exact-acme-testimonials/index.liquid with section settings for heading, blocks for cards, fluid_attributes, BEM CSS, responsive grid, presets.
assertions: Creates sections/exact-acme-testimonials/index.liquid
Uses section.settings for heading
Uses blocks for repeating testimonial cards
Includes {{ section.fluid_attributes }} on root
Includes {{ block.fluid_attributes }} on each block
CSS uses BEM naming with scoped style block
Responsive grid with mobile-first breakpoints
Presets include all 4 testimonial cards with content
Uses range instead of number for any numeric settings

evals.prompt: Clone the entire yellowbirdfoods.com site into my Fluid theme. Prefix is 'yb', Fluid token PT-xxx, Firecrawl key fc-xxx, Fluid URL https://test.fluid.app.
evals.expected_output: Should enter full site clone mode. Discover all pages via nav scrape. Build page manifest. Identify shared sections. Clone in order: nav/footer, homepage, products, collections, static pages. Upload everything to Fluid. Then run the dev-preview visual verification across each cloned page at three breakpoints before declaring the site complete.
assertions: Validates all credentials first
Runs the local toolchain preflight (Node, fluid CLI, auth, Playwright)
Scrapes homepage for navigation structure
Builds a complete page manifest
Identifies shared sections across pages
Clones nav and footer first
Clones homepage before other pages
Handles product pages with main_product preserved
Reuses sections where possible
Uploads all theme files to Fluid API
Spins up `fluid theme dev` once for the whole verification pass
Runs Playwright visual diff against EACH cloned page (homepage, products, collections, static pages)
Tests all three breakpoints (desktop, tablet, mobile) on every page
Walks each page section-by-section
Classifies findings as auto-fix vs flag-for-user per page
Surfaces flagged findings to the user before declaring the site complete

evals.prompt: I've finished building my theme locally at /tmp/fluid-theme-test/. Push it to my Fluid store at https://p.fluid.app. My token is PT-xxx.
evals.expected_output: Should create or find an application theme, then walk the directory and PUT each file as a theme resource. Text files uploaded directly, binary files via DAM first.
assertions: Creates or finds application theme via POST /api/application_themes
Walks the theme directory recursively
Uploads .liquid/.css/.js/.json as text via PUT with content field
Uploads binary files (.png/.jpg/.woff2) to DAM first via upload.fluid.app
Uses dam_asset field for binary resources
Reports theme ID and upload results

evals.prompt: Initialize a new Fluid theme working directory for cloning example.com. Prefix: ex.
evals.expected_output: Should create /tmp/fluid-theme-example/ and copy the base theme scaffolding from the skill directory. If base-theme/ is empty, should create minimum scaffolding manually.
assertions: Creates working directory at /tmp/fluid-theme-example/
Attempts to copy from ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/base-theme/
Falls back to creating scaffolding manually if base-theme is empty
Creates layouts/theme.liquid
Creates sections/main_navbar/ and sections/main_footer/
Creates config/settings_schema.json and settings_data.json
Does not modify sections/main_product/

evals.prompt: I'm cloning yellowbirdfoods.com but I don't have Playwright installed yet — just the fluid CLI. Token is PT-xxx, Firecrawl is fc-xxx, Fluid URL https://test.fluid.app, prefix yb.
evals.expected_output: Should run the toolchain preflight, detect Playwright + Chromium are missing, surface the exact install commands, and ask the user for explicit consent before installing. Should NOT silently install. If user declines, should fall back to a single full-page screenshot pass and note the missing capability in the final report.
assertions: Runs the local toolchain preflight (node, fluid CLI, auth, Playwright)
Detects Playwright and/or Chromium are missing
Surfaces the exact install command (npm install -g playwright && npx playwright install chromium)
Asks the user for explicit yes/no consent before running installs
Does not run npm install or npx playwright install without consent
If the user opts out, falls back to a reduced visual check
Notes the missing Playwright capability in the final report
Does not silently skip the visual verification step

evals.prompt: I just finished cloning the homepage and pushed it. The hero looks darker on my Fluid store than on the source site. Source is https://example.com, my Fluid theme is at /Users/me/themes/example. How do I check?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the dev-preview visual diff workflow: run `fluid theme dev` from the theme directory, then run the Playwright diff script comparing the source URL to http://127.0.0.1:9292/. Should describe the auto-fix vs flag classification, and that color mismatches are auto-fix territory. Should reference dev-preview-visual-diff.md for the canonical script.
assertions: Tells the user to run `fluid theme dev` from the theme directory
Mentions the localhost URL is http://127.0.0.1:9292/
Explains the Playwright diff script with paired source/localhost screenshots
Notes the script captures desktop, tablet, and mobile in one run
Classifies background color mismatch as an auto-fix finding (not a flag)
References references/dev-preview-visual-diff.md as the canonical protocol
Mentions the watcher-driven save → upload → hot-reload loop

evals.prompt: I cloned a homepage. The Playwright diff shows the hero photo is different — source has a farm photo, mine has a placeholder. Heading font-size is also off by 8px. What do you do?
evals.expected_output: Should classify the two findings differently: heading font-size is auto-fix (numeric, mechanical), apply directly. Hero photo swap is FLAG (image asset judgement call), surface to user before changing. Should not auto-fix the photo without confirmation.
assertions: Classifies heading font-size mismatch as auto-fix
Applies the font-size fix directly to the section file
Classifies hero photo asset swap as FLAG (do not auto-fix)
Surfaces the photo asset finding to the user before changing it
Does not silently swap the placeholder for a different image
Asks whether the user wants to upload the source photo to DAM or keep the placeholder
